name: Market Salad with Parmesan Brittle- Buon'appetito
recipeInstructions:
Use lettuces with strong flavor to complement the rich, sharp taste of the brittle and the tangy dressing. Arugula is a peppery leafy green similar in flavor to watercress. If arugula is unavailable, substitute watercress.
1. Tear lettuce into bite-sized pieces and place in a large bowl.
2. (Vinaigrette) In a separate bowl, whisk together vinegar, mustard, and garlic. Slowly whisk in olive oil until emulsified. Toss dressing with salad.
3. Divide salad among 6 plates and top each with 2 parmesan brittle pieces. Serve remaining brittle alongside the salad.
PARMESAN BRITTLE
1. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Using a food processor or knife, combine butter and flour until crumbly. Grate Parmesan cheese if possible for better texture. Mix in paprika, black pepper, and salt. Form mixture into a ball.
2. On a floured surface, roll dough into a rectangle about 12x7 inches (30x18 cm). Cut into 1-inch (2.5 cm) strips. Arrange strips on a baking sheet.
3. Bake for 9-10 minutes or until lightly golden. Allow to cool slightly before serving with salad. (Makes 10-12 brittle pieces)
